title: Week 8 Hands On Activity 2 GenSpark Website Building Guide
body:
Week 8 Hands-On Activity 2
                    Building a Website Using GenSpark AI

Overview:
In this hands-on activity, you will use GenSpark, an AI-powered platform, to build a complete
website without writing any code. GenSpark demonstrates how agentic AI can automate
complex multi-step tasks such as web design, content creation, layout planning, and
deployment.

This activity illustrates key agentic design patterns: Planning (GenSpark decides website
structure, pages, and content hierarchy), Tool Use (the AI generates code, layouts, content,
and responsive designs), and Reflection (GenSpark iterates based on your feedback and
requests). By observing these patterns in action, you'll understand how multi-agent systems
can automate complex creative workflows.

What is GenSpark?
GenSpark is an AI-powered website builder that uses agentic AI to help users create
professional websites, documents and workflows. When you provide a description of your
desired website, GenSpark's AI agent:

• Plans the website structure (pages, navigation, layout)

• Generates relevant content (text, headings, calls-to-action)

• Designs the visual layout (colours, fonts, spacing)

• Creates responsive pages that work on desktop and mobile

• Iterates based on your feedback (Reflection pattern)

This makes GenSpark an excellent example of how agentic AI systems combine multiple
design patterns to accomplish complex tasks autonomously.

Prerequisites:
• A web browser (Chrome, Firefox, or Edge recommended)

• A Google account (for signing in to GenSpark)

• A business concept or idea for your website (can be fictional)

Step 1: Sign In Using Your Google Account
• Open your web browser and navigate to https://www.genspark.ai/

• Click Sign In with Google (or the Google login button)
 • Select your Google account and authorize GenSpark to access your account

• You will be directed to the GenSpark dashboard

Step 2: Choose AI Developer; and Simple Website and Web App;
• From the GenSpark dashboard, click or select AI Developer;

• Choose the Simple Website and Web App option

• This will launch the project creation interface

Step 3: Input Your Website Prompt
GenSpark asks you to describe the website you want to build. Provide a detailed prompt
describing your website concept, target audience, features, and style preferences.

Example prompt: Create a professional website for a small bakery business called Golden
Crust Bakery located in Sydney. The website should have a home page with a hero banner,
a menu page showcasing artisan breads and pastries, an about us page telling the bakery
story, and a contact page with location and ordering information. Use warm, inviting colours
and modern typography.

• Enter your prompt describing the website you want to build

• Click Generate or Create to let the AI agent build your website

• Review the AI-generated website structure, layout, content, and design decisions

• Observe how the AI has applied Planning (website structure), Tool Use (code and content
   generation), and Design choices (visual layout)

Step 4: Follow the API Instructions and Publish Your Website
After the AI generates your website, you will be provided with instructions for inserting an API
key (if required) to complete the deployment process. Follow these steps to publish your live
website:

• Follow the on-screen instructions for any API key insertion or configuration

• Click Publish to deploy your website to the live GenSpark domain

• Your published website URL will be provided (example:
   https://lkacwuvx.gensparkspace.com/ )

Acknowledgement:

Contents was developed with assistance from Claude (Anthropic, 2026), an AI assistant
   used for research, writing, and idea generation.

Reference: Anthropic (2026) Claude [Large language model]. Available at: https://claude.ai.
